profile_bio.description: Coding Dinosaur • Entrepreneur • 200 projects • DM for collabs ➡️ https://t.co/fvuWg2duU6

tweets.text:
The most interesting part is not just the benchmark numbers.

It’s that BitCPM-CANN shows a full 1.58-bit training stack running natively on Ascend infrastructure, with weights + training scripts released openly.

Worth testing if you care about low-bit LLMs, edge AI, or non-CUDA infra:

🤗 Hugging Face：
https://t.co/wCeNVea3Sv

🔭 ModelScope:
https://t.co/Se2y0PaXwP

profile_bio.description: Coding Dinosaur • Entrepreneur • 200 projects • DM for collabs ➡️ https://t.co/fvuWg2duU6

tweets.text:
This matters because memory is one of the biggest bottlenecks for deploying AI outside big cloud setups.

Lower-bit models could make more capable AI easier to run on:

→ phones
→ PCs
→ automotive chips
→ edge devices

That means lower latency, lower cost, and more local AI use cases.

Below you can see how much memory the BitCPM4-CANN-0.5B-gguf consumes running in Ollama on my Mac.

profile_bio.description: Coding Dinosaur • Entrepreneur • 200 projects • DM for collabs ➡️ https://t.co/fvuWg2duU6

tweets.text:
The main technical takeaway:

BitCPM-CANN claims around 6× memory savings compared to BF16 while retaining 93%–99% of the full-precision MiniCPM4 baseline performance across the 1B–8B models.

The 0.5B model retains 90.1%.

Smaller memory footprint while still maintaining useful capabilities.

profile_bio.description: Coding Dinosaur • Entrepreneur • 200 projects • DM for collabs ➡️ https://t.co/fvuWg2duU6

tweets.text:
The release includes 4 model sizes:

→ 0.5B
→ 1B
→ 3B
→ 8B

And it’s not only model weights.

It also open-sourced the QAT continual pre-training and SFT scripts, which run on both NPU and GPU—making this more useful for reproducibility and future research on low-bit training.

profile_bio.description: Coding Dinosaur • Entrepreneur • 200 projects • DM for collabs ➡️ https://t.co/fvuWg2duU6

tweets.text:
Everyone is trying to make LLMs bigger.

BitCPM-CANN is interesting because it goes in the opposite direction:

a 1.58-bit ternary LLM family designed to drastically reduce memory usage while keeping useful performance.

Not "2-bit".

1.58-bit ternary.
